conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm your therapist. Everything we discuss here is confidential, so please feel comfortable.
Thanks, I appreciate that.
So you had a pretty normal commute, that's great. Can you tell me about your daily routine? Do anything exciting or different during the weekdays?
Yeah, same old same old. Work, sleep, eat, repeat. Nothing too out of the ordinary.
That sounds like a good foundation. How's work been treating you lately? Any challenges or stressors at the police station?
You know, just the usual. Paperwork, politics, and trying to keep up with all the new protocols.
I can imagine that's exhausting. Do you have any downtime or relaxation time during the day?
Not really. When I'm not on duty, I'm usually catching up on paperwork at home or watching TV.
Okay, so it sounds like your work-life balance might be a bit out of whack. Have you been sleeping well at all?
Honestly? No. I've been waking up multiple times a night and having trouble falling back asleep.
That must be really affecting your energy levels too. How have you been handling your days since that started?
It's like my batteries are just drained all the time. I struggle to get out of bed in the morning, let alone tackle tasks at work.
I understand. Sometimes when we're feeling low on energy, our mood can suffer too. How have you been feeling emotionally lately?
Honestly, pretty downbeat. Everything feels like an effort, and I just want to crawl back into bed.
That sounds really tough. Have you noticed any changes in your appetite or eating habits recently?
Yeah, my stomach's been growling a lot more often than usual, but the food doesn't seem to agree with me.
Okay, so it sounds like your body might be trying to compensate for some underlying discomfort. Can you tell me about any hobbies or activities that bring you joy these days?
Honestly? Nothing really. I used to enjoy playing basketball, but lately, even that seems too much effort.
That sounds really disappointing. How have you been handling stressful situations at work or in your personal life?
Like I said, just trying to power through and pretend everything's fine. But it's not fine.
It sounds like there might be some guilt or self-criticism creeping in. Can you tell me more about what that looks like for you?
Yeah... I keep thinking I should be doing more, being more productive, but honestly? I just feel like a failure.
Okay, well let's work on reframing that thought process. Have you noticed any physical sensations or body feelings that might be related to these emotions?
Just general fatigue and muscle tension, especially in my shoulders and neck.
Okay, so it sounds like your body is holding onto some stress too. Are there any recent events or triggers that might have contributed to this sense of burnout?
Just the usual stuff – paperwork, politics, and trying to keep up with all the new protocols at work.
I see. Well, it's great that you're acknowledging these struggles. Can you tell me about your support system? Do you have friends or family who can offer a listening ear?
Yeah, my partner is super supportive, but even they get overwhelmed sometimes.
That makes sense. What are some coping strategies you've used in the past when feeling stressed or overwhelmed?
Honestly? Nothing really effective. Just trying to push through and pretend everything's fine.
Well, let's work on finding some healthier coping mechanisms together. What are your goals for our sessions today?
Just to feel less like I'm stuck in quicksand, you know? To get my feet back under me somehow.
That's a great place to start. How do you think we can achieve that goal in the coming weeks?
Honestly? I don't know. But it's worth trying, right?
Absolutely. Before we wrap up today, is there anything on your mind or anything you'd like to get off your chest?
Just... I guess just wanting someone to understand what I'm going through.
Of course, and I'm here to listen. That's all for today – thanks for opening up so much!
